verses.text: Sl 35 Neste lamento, o servo aflito do Senhor pede que Deus veja sua situação e o vindique.
Sl 35 Neste lamento, o servo aflito do Senhor pede que Deus veja sua situação e o vindique.
Sl 35 Neste lamento, o servo aflito do Senhor pede que Deus veja sua situação e o vindique.
35.4-10 O salmista anseia pela salvação do Senhor. A linguagem parece vingativa, mas brota do desejo de justiça e de que o Senhor cuide do necessitado e do oprimido.
35.4-10 O salmista anseia pela salvação do Senhor. A linguagem parece vingativa, mas brota do desejo de justiça e de que o Senhor cuide do necessitado e do oprimido.
35.6 Caminho escuro e escorregadio sugere instabilidade e ruína intempestiva sob o juízo do Senhor (69.23; 73.18; 82.5; 107.10; 143.3). • O Anjo do Senhor é agente de juízo, além de salvação.

35:7 net in a pit—or, "pit of their net"—or, "net-pit," as "holy hill" for "hill of holiness" (Ps 2:6); a figure from hunting (Ps 7:15). Their imprecations on impenitent rebels against God need no vindication; His justice and wrath are for such; His mercy for penitents. Compare Ps 7:16; Ps 11:5, on the peculiar fate of the wicked here noticed.

35:8 Let destruction come upon him at unawares,.... Or a "storm" (r), such as is caused in the eastern countries by a south wind, very sudden, violent, and destructive (s): the singular number being here used, some Jewish commentators, as Kimchi, have thought Saul is particularly meant; and some Christian interpreters have been of opinion that Judas is intended: the imprecations here may be compared with those which respect him, Ps 109:6. Though this may regard every one of the enemies of David, or of Christ and his people, whose ruin and destruction will come upon them unawares;

35:9 And my soul shall be joyful in the Lord,.... Not in the destruction of his enemies, but in the God of his salvation; the Targum is, "in the Word of the Lord"; the essential Word of God, the promised Messiah, Saviour, and Redeemer. Christ is the object of a believer's joy; he rejoices in his person, as he is the mighty God, able to save him, and to keep what he has committed to him, and to preserve him from falling;
35.10 Nada no universo se compara ao Senhor (86.8; 89.6,8; Is 40.18,25).

35.11-18 O salmista apresenta ao Senhor as razões do seu sofrimento. O mal tem muitas faces. Está cansado e abatido e sabe que só o Senhor pode socorrê-lo. 35.11 Testemunhas maliciosas só querem violência (11.4-6; 54.3).

35.16 Os agressores do salmista rangem os dentes para expressar inveja amarga.

35:17 Lord, how long wilt thou look on?.... And behold these injuries and insults, and not arise to help and save? The psalmist firmly believed the omniscience of God, and was well assured he saw all that was done; but he was ready to consider him only as a spectator; or, however, seems impatient until he arose and showed himself strong on his behalf; see Ps 35:22; rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions; his "soul" and his "darling" mean the same;

35:18 I will give thee thanks in the great congregation,.... This is the resolution the psalmist came unto; the promise he made, that should he be delivered from his enemies, he would give God thanks in the most public manner; that is, he would acknowledge God to be the author of the mercy, and himself unworthy of it; and would ascribe glory, honour, blessing, and thanksgiving to him, in the midst of the church and people of God; they joining with him in it, when he should be restored to an attendance with them he before prays for;

35.19-25 Nesta oração por vindicação, a crise do salmista é tão grande que a solução só pode estar no Senhor. O salmista escolheu o caminho elevado, sabendo que a vingança pertence ao Senhor. A paciência de Deus endurece o coração dos ímpios e lhes dá motivos para justificar o estilo de vida perverso. 35.19 João aplicou essa declaração de inocência (cf. 69.4) a Jesus (Jo 15.25).

35.21,25 Aha!… Eis: ambas as palavras traduzem a mesma expressão de júbilo (hebraico *he’akh*).

35.26-28 O salmista convoca o Senhor a julgar os ímpios e a livrar os piedosos, trazendo-lhes alegria.
